nativeRange.habitat: Cultivar/wild form of Microsorum pteropus from Southeast Asian rainforest streams, riverbanks, and waterfalls, where the parent species grows epiphytically on submerged or emergent rocks and wood; the Trident form has been observed in the wild during fish-collection trips in Borneo

imageCandidates.notes: Highest-resolution (1280x1280) product image from primary source page. Clean isolated specimen showing the diagnostic narrow, forked trident-shaped leaf tips that distinguish this variety from the smooth-bladed type form.
Medium-resolution (500x659) variant of the same product image.

summary: A narrow-leaved cultivar/wild form of Java Fern with distinctive forked, trident-shaped leaf tips. Same forgiving epiphytic care as the parent species — rhizome must stay above substrate, attached to wood or rock — but stays smaller than the type form and is prized as a midground-to-background accent.
careNotes: Microsorum pteropus var. Trident (commonly called Trident Java Fern) is a narrow-leaved variety of the standard Java Fern, distinguished by long, slender fronds whose tips fork into a characteristic two- or three-pronged trident shape as the leaves mature. Sources differ on its origin: it is sometimes described as a hobbyist-selected cultivar and sometimes as a naturally-occurring wild form (Castle Dawn Aquatics reports observing it during fish-collection trips in Borneo). Plants stay slightly smaller than the type form — typically 15–25 cm tall — and the slimmer fronds give it a more delicate, flowing visual texture, making it a popular midground-to-background accent in aquascapes. CRITICAL husbandry rule (shared with all Java Fern variants): the horizontal rhizome MUST stay above the substrate. Burying it causes rot and plant death. Attach the plant to driftwood, porous rock, or other hardscape with thread, fishing line, or cyanoacrylate gel until roots anchor naturally (typically several weeks). As an epiphyte, it absorbs nutrients primarily through its leaves and rhizome from the water column, so liquid fertilization is generally more useful than substrate dosing; CO2 and fertilization noticeably encourage fuller growth. Lighting is forgiving: low light is sufficient, and excessive light tends to encourage algae on the slow-growing fronds. Water-parameter tolerance is unusually broad and inherited from the parent species — sources document healthy growth across pH 5.5–7.5, gH 2–15, kH 0–14, and temperatures roughly 20–28 °C (68–82 °F). Growth rate is generally reported as slow (Modern Aquarium), though some sources describe Trident as one of the more prolific Java Fern variants (Castle Dawn Aquatics); under good conditions it can become a focal point of an aquascape relatively quickly. Like the parent species, Trident is not eaten by most herbivorous fish, so it pairs well with goldfish and African cichlid setups that would shred softer plants. Small dark spots on leaf undersides are sporangia (reproductive structures), not disease. Other Java Fern variants (Windelov, Narrow Leaf, Needle Leaf, Philippine) have or will have separate species entries; the standard type form is fw-plant-019.
breedingNotes: Propagated through the same two methods as the parent species: (1) rhizome division — cut a section of healthy rhizome with several leaves and roots and re-attach to new hardscape; (2) adventitious plantlets — mature leaves spontaneously produce small daughter plants along their edges, which can be detached once they have a few leaves and trailing roots and attached to new hardscape. Plantlet production accelerates dramatically on stressed or dying leaves. True spore reproduction is possible from emersed fronds but rarely occurs in submerged aquarium conditions.

primary.name: Modern Aquarium
primary.notes: Primary source for temperature (68–82 °F ≈ 20–28 °C), low light, CO2 not required (beneficial), slow growth rate, midground-to-background placement, easy care level, the distinctive narrow forked trident-shaped leaf tips that distinguish this variety, and the critical rhizome-above-substrate rule. Also provided the product images used as imageCandidates.

additional.name: Castle Dawn Aquatics
additional.notes: Cross-checked the long, narrow, forking leaf description; noted that Trident is reported as one of the more prolific Java Fern variants (contrast with Modern Aquarium's slow rating); confirmed it has been observed in the wild in Borneo, and that it follows general Java Fern parameters (pH 4.0–7.5, 18–28 °C, low-to-moderate light, hardscape attachment).

additional.name: The Aquarium Guide
additional.notes: Cross-checked the forked-leaf description distinguishing Trident from Needle Leaf, Narrow Leaf, and Windelov variants; confirmed general Java Fern care applies (68–82 °F, pH 6–7.5, low lighting, hardscape attachment).

additional.name: AquaMate parent entry: Java Fern (fw-plant-019)
additional.notes: Inherited water-parameter tolerance ranges (pH 5.5–7.5, gH 2–15, kH 0–14), epiphyte/rhizome care rules, family/order (Polypodiaceae/Polypodiales), and Southeast Asian native-range data from the parent species entry. Trident is a variety of Microsorum pteropus, so parent-species values apply except where Trident-specific sources differ (notably the slightly smaller adult size and the narrow forked leaf morphology).
